Hero.name: Kanta Chen
Hero.tagline: Senior frontend engineer at Momentx. I break abstract or complex requirements into clear architecture and actionable plans, then keep refining structure, performance and interaction until a product goes from usable to excellent.

AboutMe.title: About Me
AboutMe.text1: I graduated in **mechanical engineering**, where I first met **C++** and studied **automation** and **IoT**. Compared with mechanical or electronic work, I fell for **programming**. I love building a complete system from scratch, one step at a time, and the satisfaction that brings. As Hayao Miyazaki put it: "The important things in life are often troublesome. If we lived in a world without troubles, even 'troubles' would become enviable." To me, programming feels like **solving puzzles** — full of challenge and delight.
AboutMe.text2: I care about **code quality** — **cleanliness**, **readability** and **maintainability** — and keep exploring how the field evolves. In development I am good at **grasping requirements quickly**, breaking abstract or complex problems into **clear logical architecture** and **actionable strategy**, so projects stay on schedule.
AboutMe.text3: On a team I **listen** and **synthesise** different viewpoints, using **structured thinking** and **clear communication** to help everyone focus on the core problem and land solutions that are both **practical** and **extensible**. I believe good working culture comes from clear **architecture** and a sustainable **development rhythm**.
AboutMe.text4: Most products get to **80%**; what really separates them is the final **20%** of detail and experience. I keep polishing **architecture**, **performance** and **interaction** until a product feels smoother, more intuitive and more professional — taking it from "usable" to "**excellent**".

Sections.aboutTitle: Who I am, and how I work
Sections.focusDesc: Sole frontend engineer at Momentx, owning architecture and core development across these three product lines.
Sections.stackTitle: What this site is built with
Sections.stackDesc: This site is a portfolio piece in itself: a hand-rolled design system, scroll choreography and full bilingual support.
Sections.contactTitle: Got something in mind? Let us talk.
Sections.contactDesc: Freelance work, full-time roles, or just a good technical conversation — my inbox is open.

1.title: AI Agent SaaS platform
1.text: A SaaS platform for customisable AI agents built with Vue 3, Nuxt.js and TypeScript — agent / prompt / memory management, multi-model LLM integration, SSE streaming, Stripe subscriptions and multi-tenant permissions.

2.title: Cross-platform AI collaboration app
2.text: A Flutter app combining chat and canvas across iOS, Android, macOS, Windows and Web, with context-aware AI responses and real-time multi-user collaboration.

3.title: Frontend architecture & performance
3.text: Led a React → Vue 3 + Nuxt rewrite, introduced SSR and SEO optimisation, and built CI/CD pipelines covering unit tests and Playwright E2E.

Stack.title: Stack
Stack.text: This is a website built with the following technologies:

0.text1: Inventory management system, company HR portal, logistics management system
0.text2: Scheduled auto-posting, algorithmic trading bot, automated Thread replies
0.text3: Online course platform (front & back office), expense-tracking app, parking admin, IoT vending machine

Analysis.title: Analyze What Fruits I Ate
Analysis.instruction: Please select a date and click the search button to view the fruits I ate.
Analysis.instructionDetail: On this page, I demonstrate how to retrieve data from the backend, transform it through the service layer, and then pass it to the presentation layer, using Chart.js for visualization.

WhackAMole.title: Whack A Mole Game
WhackAMole.instruction: Click the moles with your mouse
WhackAMole.instructionDetail: In this game, I used a recursive approach to set setTimeout, allowing the moles to continuously appear randomly. After hitting a mole, the setTimeout is reset to solve the issue of continuous mole movement.

MazeGame.title: Maze Game
MazeGame.instruction: Please use the direction keys to move
MazeGame.instructionDetail: I used the Depth-First Search (DFS) algorithm to generate the maze. Starting from the star, it continuously delves along a path until it can no longer proceed, then backtracks to the nearest branching point to continue generating other paths. This method effectively creates a random and solvable maze.
